meaning: n.同等的人,贵族vi.凝视,窥视vt.与...同等,封为贵族
examples: She gets on well with her peer group.
Teenagers may find it difficult to resist peer pressure.
Climbing steep stairs to the choir gallery you peer into a cavernous interior.
Youth participate in peer-mentorship activities and are encouraged to write and rap about their feelings.
This is to allow enough time for the peer review process to take place.
